mnemonics: Garbage In, Gospel Out: Master Data Management (MDM) ensures that the 'gospel' (single source of truth) comes from clean, reliable data, preventing garbage outputs from inconsistent sources.
keyPoints: MDM creates a single, authoritative source of master data (like customer, product, employee) for the entire organization.
It synchronizes master data across different systems and applications to ensure consistency.
Key processes include data consolidation, cleansing, and governance to maintain data quality and integrity.
commonMistakes: Treating MDM as a one-time project instead of an ongoing program requiring continuous governance and stewardship.
Focusing only on the technical integration of systems while neglecting the crucial change management and data ownership aspects.
